free_text: Measurement of formation cross sections producing
short-lived nuclei by 14 MeV neutrons - Pr, Ba, Ce,
Symposium on Nuclear Data, Nov. 16-17, JAERI,Tokai
Experimental details and the details of the correction
The d-beam energy was 300 keV and the intensity
was 5 milliAmps. A sample transport system of six
pneumatic tubes at angles of 0, 50, 75, 105, 125 and
155 deg to the deuteron beam provided irradiation
14. MeV. The transfer time was about 2 sec. Another
tube was put at -105 deg to check the symmetry of the
system. The distance between the tritium target and
the irradiation position was 15 cm. An additional
tube set at zero angle and at 1.5 cm distance was use
when high neutron flux was required. The effective
reaction neutron energy at the irradiation position
was determined by the ratio of the 90-Zr(n,2n) and
93-Nb(n,2n) cross sections. The uncertainty in the
neutron energy is estimated to be 90 keV.
.The powder samples with dimensions 10 mm x 10 mm and
1 mm thickness wrapped in powder paper. Either
separated isotopes or natural samples were used.
.The neutron flux at the irradiation positions was
determined with two aluminium monitor foils,
Two Ge detectors with efficiencies of 12 and 1%
at an equivalent distance 5 cm. The error in the
efficiency curve is estimated to be 5, 3 and 1.5%
in the energy region below 80 keV, 80 - 300 keV
irradiation position measurement Based on the
.The data were corrected for time fluctuation of the
neutron flux, contribution of scattered low-energy
neutrons, true coincidence sum, random coincidence
sum, thickness of samples, self absorption of gamma-
correction for random coincidence sum less then 0.4%
correction for neutron flux fluctuation less then
systematic errors as given below till ERR-2
* secondary reference cross section error for
* uncertainty in correction for low  energy
Total error was derived from the sum of
estimated uncertainty in En, no details given
